audit_text: KNOWN_EXECUTOR_MODELS divergence — backend has frozenset({"sonnet","haiku","opus","opusplan"}) in constants.py; frontend list is ["sonnet","haiku","opus"] — no "opusplan". If "opusplan" lands in DB as default_model, the Defaults <select> has no matching <option>; browser silently shows first option, next user change overwrites the value without warning. Distinct defect class — constants drift backend↔frontend.
verdict_evidence: constants.py:235 declares KNOWN_EXECUTOR_MODELS as frozenset({"sonnet","haiku","opus","opusplan"}); frontend/src/lib/config.ts:1385-1389 declares it as [SONNET, HAIKU, OPUS] only — opusplan absent. DefaultsSection.svelte:223 iterates KNOWN_EXECUTOR_MODELS to build options; line 74 binds prefModel = prefs.default_model. With a DB row holding default_model="opusplan" the bound value lacks a matching option and the next change silently overwrites.
acceptance_criteria: frontend KNOWN_EXECUTOR_MODELS includes "opusplan" alongside sonnet/haiku/opus
an EXECUTOR_MODEL_OPUSPLAN named export exists for symmetry with the other three
NEW_SESSION_STRINGS.executorLabels has a label entry for opusplan
MODEL_INPUT_RATES_USD_PER_MILLION has an opusplan entry (or doc comment explaining its absence)
DefaultsSection select renders an opusplan option (visible in vitest assertion or e2e snapshot)
frontend NewSessionForm.test.ts executor option list assertion is updated to include opusplan or explicitly justifies its exclusion
executor_brief: Add "opusplan" to frontend KNOWN_EXECUTOR_MODELS in frontend/src/lib/config.ts (line 1385-1389) so the constant matches the backend frozenset at src/bearings/config/constants.py:235. Mirror the existing EXECUTOR_MODEL_* named export pattern, then update NEW_SESSION_STRINGS.executorLabels and MODEL_INPUT_RATES_USD_PER_MILLION (line 1432) to carry an opusplan entry. Update frontend/src/lib/components/new_session/__tests__/NewSessionForm.test.ts:89 (and any sibling tests asserting the option list) to include opusplan.

audit_text: display_name max-length not enforced at PATCH boundary. DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH=120 exists in constants and is applied in sync_from_system, but PreferencesPatch.display_name has no validator. A direct PATCH with a 10k-char name stores it verbatim. Fix: Annotated[str | None, Field(max_length=DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH)] = None. Same shape as #1 finding 2 / #5 finding 1 — POST↔PATCH validator drift.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/preferences.md §"Profile / Identity" → "Display name field — freetext input, max 120 characters"
verdict_evidence: src/bearings/web/models/preferences.py:48 declares display_name: str | None = None — no Field(max_length=…) annotation. The route at src/bearings/web/routes/preferences.py:132 forwards body.display_name unchanged into prefs_db.patch_preferences without length checking. Only sync_from_system at line 277 truncates to DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH. The DB column is plain TEXT (sqlite, no length constraint). A PATCH body with display_name of arbitrary length is therefore persisted verbatim.
acceptance_criteria: PreferencesPatch.display_name uses Annotated[str | None, Field(max_length=DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH)] = None (or pydantic StringConstraints with max_length=DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH)
PATCH /api/preferences with display_name length DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH+1 returns 422
PATCH /api/preferences with display_name of exactly DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH characters succeeds with 200
No inline literal — the validator references DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH from bearings.config.constants
existing test_patch_preferences_sets_display_name and test_patch_preferences_clears_display_name still pass unchanged
executor_brief: Add Field(max_length=DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH) to PreferencesPatch.display_name at src/bearings/web/models/preferences.py:48. Import DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH from bearings.config.constants — no inline literal. Use Pydantic v2 Annotated[str | None, Field(max_length=DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH)] = None. Add two tests to tests/test_preferences_avatar_api.py: a 422 case with len(name)==DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH+1 and a 200 case at exactly DISPLAY_NAME_MAX_LENGTH.

audit_text: notify_on_complete: bool | None = None is too permissive. Column is NOT NULL DEFAULT 0. Sending {"notify_on_complete": null} is silently eaten by the is not None guard. Should be bool = False — Pydantic v2 model_fields_set still captures explicitly-set fields even when value equals default.
verdict_evidence: src/bearings/web/models/preferences.py:50 declares notify_on_complete: bool | None = None. The route at src/bearings/web/routes/preferences.py:133 forwards body.notify_on_complete into the DB layer with model_fields_set as the field gate. src/bearings/db/preferences.py:145 reads `if "notify_on_complete" in fields and notify_on_complete is not None:` — the second clause silently drops a caller's explicit null. The schema column is NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, so null is never legitimate input.
acceptance_criteria: PreferencesPatch.notify_on_complete is typed bool = False (or bool with default False, no None)
PATCH /api/preferences with {"notify_on_complete": null} returns 422
PATCH /api/preferences with {"notify_on_complete": false} sets the column to 0
PATCH /api/preferences with {"notify_on_complete": true} sets the column to 1
db/preferences.py patch_preferences signature for notify_on_complete drops the | None and the corresponding `is not None` guard
existing test_patch_notify_on_complete_true / _false / _omit_leaves_unchanged tests still pass
executor_brief: Tighten PreferencesPatch.notify_on_complete to bool = False at src/bearings/web/models/preferences.py:50. Drop the | None typing and the corresponding `is not None` guard at src/bearings/db/preferences.py:145; the field-set guard alone (`"notify_on_complete" in fields`) governs writes. Pydantic v2 model_fields_set still captures explicitly-set fields equal to the default, so existing PATCH tests pass unchanged. Add a 422 test for {"notify_on_complete": null} to tests/test_preferences_notify_api.py.

audit_text: No test suite for the four base fields (theme, default_model, default_permission_mode, default_working_dir). test_preferences_avatar_api.py and test_preferences_notify_api.py exist. Nothing covers: correct defaults on GET, PATCH updates, null clears a nullable field, omit leaves unchanged.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/preferences.md §"Settings → Defaults" — theme, default_model, default_permission_mode, default_working_dir
verdict_evidence: `grep -rn "default_permission_mode\|default_working_dir\|default_model" tests/` shows references in test_preferences_notify_api.py:128 (field-presence loop only — `field in payload`), tests/test_db_schema.py and tests/test_migrate_v0_17_to_v0_18.py (DB-shape only), but no PATCH-behavior test exercising those fields. tests/test_preferences_avatar_api.py covers display_name + avatar only. tests/test_preferences_notify_api.py covers notify_on_complete only.
acceptance_criteria: new test file tests/test_preferences_base_fields_api.py exists
GET /api/preferences returns the schema-default values for theme / default_model / default_permission_mode / default_working_dir on a freshly-loaded DB
PATCH /api/preferences with {theme: "<known>"} updates theme; subsequent GET round-trips
PATCH /api/preferences with {default_model: "sonnet"} sets the column; GET reflects it
PATCH /api/preferences with {default_model: null} clears a previously-set value
PATCH /api/preferences with {default_permission_mode: "<known>"} round-trips
PATCH /api/preferences with {default_working_dir: "/wd"} round-trips; null clears it
PATCH /api/preferences with {} returns 200 and no field is mutated
PATCH /api/preferences with {theme: "<x>"} leaves default_model unchanged
each test reuses the existing app_client / sandboxed-DB fixture pattern from test_preferences_notify_api.py
tests do not import or read v0.17.x source per repo invariants
executor_brief: Author tests/test_preferences_base_fields_api.py covering GET defaults and PATCH update / null-clear / omit-unchanged for theme, default_model, default_permission_mode, default_working_dir. Mirror the sandboxed-DB fixture pattern from tests/test_preferences_notify_api.py:30-52. Use schema defaults (read from src/bearings/db/schema.sql) — do not hard-code values that duplicate constants. Pre-dispatch signal noted: orchestrator flagged pre-existing pytest failures in preferences.py / reorg.py route-naming. Static reading of the routes and tests surfaced no naming-convention test or operation_id collision; if you encounter a failure during test development, diagnose and fix as part of this finding. Per V1_FEATURE_AUDIT and runbook, the test additions are the deliverable — do not regress existing test_preferences_avatar_api / test_preferences_notify_api.

audit_text: DB theme column is write-only from the UI. Defaults section writes it on change; boot path initializes from localStorage only (themeStore). refreshPreferences() on layout mount only populates displayName/avatarUrl/cacheBust — not theme. DB value is future-proofing but currently dead for hydration.
verdict_evidence: preferencesStore.applyPreferences (lines 74-78) writes only displayName / avatarUrl / cacheBust; the Out shape's `theme` field is read but discarded. DefaultsSection.svelte:72-73 explicitly comments: "Theme is not loaded from DB here; themeStore.theme already reflects the persisted localStorage value." The DB theme column is therefore write-only from the SPA — it is set on change in Defaults but never read on layout boot.
acceptance_criteria: spec is reconciled: either (a) DB hydration of theme is added on layout mount with documented precedence (DB > localStorage on first load, localStorage on subsequent loads, or similar), or (b) the DB theme column is documented as write-only future-proofing and the docs/behavior/preferences.md §"Theme field cross-sync" section is updated to say so
whichever path is chosen, behavior is documented in docs/behavior/preferences.md and docs/behavior/themes.md
no inline literals are introduced — all new strings live in config.ts string tables
executor_brief: This is a design observation flagged in the audit. Pick a path: (a) wire DB-theme hydration into the layout-mount path so first-load on a new device reads the user's persisted theme, with documented precedence in docs/behavior/preferences.md and docs/behavior/themes.md; or (b) document the column as deliberate future-proofing and clarify in the spec that the DB theme is not read on hydration. Mark whichever decision and land it consistently in code and docs.

audit_text: RoutingRulesSection has inline string literals for heading and lede — every other section uses config.ts string tables. Low stakes but inconsistent.
verdict_evidence:
RoutingRulesSection.svelte:17 uses aria-label="System routing rules" inline; line 18 has

System routing rules

inline; lines 20-23 carry an inline lede paragraph. The other settings sections (e.g. DefaultsSection.svelte uses PREFERENCES_STRINGS, HelpSection uses HELP_SECTION_STRINGS) source these strings from config.ts.
acceptance_criteria: a ROUTING_RULES_SECTION_STRINGS (or matching name following the existing convention) entry exists in frontend/src/lib/config.ts with heading, ariaLabel, and lede fields
RoutingRulesSection.svelte references those keys via {ROUTING_RULES_SECTION_STRINGS.heading} etc — no inline literal text remains in the markup
existing svelte-check / vitest suites pass unmodified
executor_brief: Move the heading, aria-label, and lede paragraph in frontend/src/lib/components/settings/sections/RoutingRulesSection.svelte:17-23 into a new string-table entry in frontend/src/lib/config.ts. Match the naming pattern used by HELP_SECTION_STRINGS / PREFERENCES_STRINGS. Wire RoutingRulesSection.svelte to consume the new keys. No behavior change.

audit_text: Theme in Appearance (ThemePicker) writes localStorage only; Defaults writes localStorage + DB. Asymmetry is intentional per spec but means Appearance changes don't persist to DB.
verdict_evidence: frontend/src/lib/themes/ThemePicker.svelte:14-20 calls only setTheme() on change. setTheme() at frontend/src/lib/themes/store.svelte.ts:68-82 calls applyThemeToDom + saveStoredTheme (localStorage only) — no PATCH. DefaultsSection.svelte:108-122 additionally calls patchField({theme: next}) so the DB row is updated. The audit flags this as 'intentional per spec' but observes the asymmetry. docs/behavior/preferences.md §"Theme field cross-sync" describes the cross-sync direction but does not explicitly address whether ThemePicker should PATCH.
acceptance_criteria: coupled with finding feature-8-005: a single decision documents whether the DB theme column is read on hydration AND whether ThemePicker writes to it. The two questions answer together
docs/behavior/preferences.md and docs/behavior/themes.md unambiguously describe ThemePicker's persistence path (localStorage-only OR localStorage + DB) and the boot-hydration path
if the chosen path is symmetric (both Appearance and Defaults PATCH the DB), ThemePicker is updated to PATCH and Appearance tests cover the round-trip
if asymmetry is preserved, the spec explicitly notes it and explains the rationale
executor_brief: Resolve the design asymmetry jointly with feature-8-005 (DB theme write-only). Decide the persistence model — localStorage-only via ThemePicker + localStorage+DB via Defaults (current behavior, document explicitly) or unify both to write the DB. Land the chosen model consistently in code and docs/behavior/preferences.md + docs/behavior/themes.md. No new inline literals.
